text:
Once upon a time in a vibrant little town, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He loved nothing more than reading books about the stars. One moonlit night, Oliver flew down to the old town library to borrow a astronomy magazine.

As he perched elegantly on a branch, he opened the magazine. Unexpectedly, it was filled with tales of incredible bird rescues. His heart warmed at the stories of courage.

Oliver wanted to help these little birds. So he decided to go rescue one himself. Late one night, wearing his nicest owl mask, he fluttered out into the garden. There was a young songbird stuck in a broken branch.

Oliver swooped down gently. "Don't worry," he whispered. With his sharp claws, he snapped off the broken piece. Then he sang a sweet song to sooth the bird.

For days, Oliver visited the bird, teaching it to sing. Together they perched on branches around the town garden. People
